title: rubus subsessilis
intro: Rubus subsessilis, also known as the Japanese bramble or mountain raspberry, is a species of flowering plant in the rose family (Rosaceae) that is native to East Asia. The medicinal properties of Rubus subsessilis include its antioxidant, anti-inflammatory, and antimicrobial properties, which have been traditionally used to treat various health conditions. The health benefits associated with Rubus subsessilis include its ability to lower blood pressure, improve cardiovascular health, and support immune function. Herbal preparations of Rubus subsessilis can be found in tea, tincture, and capsule forms, and are often used to promote overall well-being and alleviate symptoms of stress and anxiety.
